scenario: Redux Toolkit Query (RTK Query) vs React Query for an existing Redux codebase.
context: App has Redux for client state. Adding server state.
alex_initial_position: RTK Query. Integrates with existing Redux. One store.
sam_initial_position: React Query. Better DX, more features.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Two libraries for state is overkill. RTK Query keeps everything in Redux.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: React Query's devtools, optimistic updates, and infinite query are better.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: RTK Query has same features. Tagged invalidation cleaner.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Many teams find React Query DX better.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For Redux teams: RTK Query. New teams: React Query.

context_dependent_answer: Redux incumbent: RTK Query. New project: React Query.
decision_framework: debate-176: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: Redux Toolkit Query (RTK Query) vs React Query for an existing Redux codebase.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors mix libraries. Senior insight: state libraries should be coherent. Don't bolt React Query onto Redux without reason.

scenario: Compound components with implicit state vs explicit state props.
alex_initial_position: Implicit state via context. Less verbose.
sam_initial_position: Explicit controlled. Forms with tabs need external state control.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Most tabs are uncontrolled. Implicit handles 90%.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Some tabs need URL-state, programmatic switching. Explicit needed.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Both: uncontrolled by default, controlled when value+onChange provided.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed. <Tabs> with optional value/onChange.

context_dependent_answer: Dual-mode: uncontrolled default, controlled when value provided.
decision_framework: debate-177: judge the component composition patterns debate in this scenario: Compound components with implicit state vs explicit state props.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors pick one mode. Senior insight: dual-mode (controlled + uncontrolled) is React's standard pattern (e.g., <input>).

scenario: Form library for React Server Components: server actions vs client form library.
sam_initial_position: RHF for client validation + server actions for submission.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Server actions handle submission. No JS needed for basic forms.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Complex validation needs client. RHF for that.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: useActionState handles server validation. No client lib needed.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For complex client UX (conditional fields, debounced validation), RHF still useful.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted. Server action for submission + RHF for complex client UX.

context_dependent_answer: RSC + simple form: server action + useActionState. Complex form: RHF + server action.
decision_framework: debate-178: judge the form library choices debate in this scenario: Form library for React Server Components: server actions vs client form library.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors over-engineer simple forms. Senior insight: server actions cover simple forms with progressive enhancement for free.

scenario: Cache persistence: persist React Query cache to localStorage for offline.
alex_initial_position: Persist with broadcastChannel for multi-tab sync.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Offline = no fetch. Need persisted cache.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Persistence has stale-data risk.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Use cache time + staleTime. Stale shown then refetched online.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted for PWA. Standard apps: skip persistence.

context_dependent_answer: PWA: persist. Non-PWA: don't persist.
decision_framework: debate-179: judge the data fetching strategy debate in this scenario: Cache persistence: persist React Query cache to localStorage for offline.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors lose state on reload. Senior insight: warm cache = perceived perf. Persist when appropriate.

scenario: @layer in CSS for cascade control. Use Tailwind's layers or define your own.
context: Mixing Tailwind utilities, component CSS, and reset CSS.
alex_initial_position: Use @layer to control cascade. Tailwind layers (base, components, utilities) + custom.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: @layer makes specificity explicit. Without it, source order matters.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Tailwind v4 handles layers internally. Don't fight it.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For mixed CSS (component lib CSS + Tailwind), layers help.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted for mixed. Otherwise unneeded.

context_dependent_answer: Mixed CSS sources: @layer. Pure Tailwind: rely on its layers.
decision_framework: debate-180: judge the styling approach wars debate in this scenario: @layer in CSS for cascade control. Use Tailwind's layers or define your own.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors fight specificity wars. Senior insight: @layer makes cascade declarative.

scenario: CSS @starting-style for entrance animations vs Framer Motion AnimatePresence.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: @starting-style is Chrome 117+. Safari and Firefox catching up.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Until universal, Framer Motion safer.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Progressive enhancement: @starting-style with Framer fallback for unsupported.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Two paths to maintain.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Or wait until 2026 when @starting-style is universal.

context_dependent_answer: Chrome-first audience: @starting-style. Cross-browser: Framer Motion. Wait if not urgent.
decision_framework: debate-181: judge the animation library decisions debate in this scenario: CSS @starting-style for entrance animations vs Framer Motion AnimatePresence.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors miss new CSS specs. Senior insight: CSS is gaining ground. Check what's possible without JS first.

scenario: Property-based testing (fast-check) vs example-based testing.
alex_initial_position: Property tests for invariants. Example tests for specific cases.
sam_initial_position: Example tests sufficient. Property tests over-engineering.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Property tests catch edge cases humans miss. fast-check generates inputs.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Property tests slow CI. Edge case bugs rare.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For pure functions with invariants (parsers, validators), property tests find bugs example tests miss.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted for pure logic. Property tests on pure logic, examples elsewhere.

context_dependent_answer: Pure logic with invariants: property. Behavior: example.
decision_framework: debate-182: judge the testing philosophy debate in this scenario: Property-based testing (fast-check) vs example-based testing.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't use property tests. Senior insight: properties test invariants - bugs found that you didn't think to check.

scenario: any vs unknown for incoming JSON.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: any disables type checking. unknown forces narrowing.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: any pragmatic at boundaries where validation runs anyway.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Zod schema returns inferred type. No any needed.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Zod parse > any.

context_dependent_answer: Use Zod. Never use any.
decision_framework: debate-183: judge the typescript strictness debate in this scenario: any vs unknown for incoming JSON.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors trust API responses. Senior insight: incoming data is unknown until parsed.

scenario: Lazy-load all images vs selective lazy-load.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: fetchpriority=high signals browser to prioritize.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Browser already prioritizes above-the-fold. Hint redundant.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For LCP image specifically, hint helps. ~100ms faster on tests.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted for LCP image. Other above-fold images: default.

context_dependent_answer: Below-fold: loading=lazy. LCP image: fetchpriority=high. Other above-fold: default.
decision_framework: debate-184: judge the performance vs dx tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Lazy-load all images vs selective lazy-load.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors lazy-load everything. Senior insight: lazy-loading above-the-fold hurts LCP.

scenario: Module federation vs npm workspaces for micro-frontends.
context: Multi-team org wants to share components without npm publish cycle.
alex_initial_position: Module federation. Runtime sharing, independent deploys.
sam_initial_position: npm workspaces + Turborepo. Build-time sharing, simpler.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Module federation enables independent team deploys.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Runtime sharing has version coordination challenges.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For genuinely independent teams (different release cadences), federation justified.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Most orgs don't have truly independent cadences. Monorepo + Turborepo simpler.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted. Most: monorepo. Truly independent: federation.

context_dependent_answer: Most orgs: monorepo. Independent teams: federation.
decision_framework: debate-185: judge the build tool choices debate in this scenario: Module federation vs npm workspaces for micro-frontends.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors adopt micro-frontends for hype. Senior insight: micro-frontends are organizational pattern, not technical. Use only when org demands.

scenario: Headless UI + own styling vs full styled library (MUI).
context: Building product, design system not mature.
sam_initial_position: MUI with theming. Fast start, refactor later.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: MUI's design opinions fight bespoke designs.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For pre-PMF startup, ship fast with MUI. Theme later.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: MUI migration later is painful. Start with what you'll keep.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For pre-PMF, perfect is enemy of shipped.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: shadcn registry is fast start AND owns the code.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. shadcn beats MUI for new projects.

context_dependent_answer: Pre-PMF or speed: shadcn. Established product: own design system on Radix. MUI: existing MUI codebases.
decision_framework: debate-186: judge the component library strategy debate in this scenario: Headless UI + own styling vs full styled library (MUI).. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors adopt full libraries. Senior insight: shadcn (or similar) gives speed + ownership.

scenario: Sentry error fingerprinting: default vs custom.
context: Many error reports, hard to triage.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Default groups by stack trace. Some errors with same root cause get split.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Custom fingerprinting requires maintenance.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For high-volume errors, custom fingerprint reduces noise.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Default for low-volume, custom for repeats.

context_dependent_answer: Default first. Custom for noisy error types.
decision_framework: debate-187: judge the error boundary strategy debate in this scenario: Sentry error fingerprinting: default vs custom.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors ignore noisy errors. Senior insight: error noise hides real bugs. Fingerprint groups.

scenario: Library bundle: single file vs multiple entry points.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Consumers import only what they use. Tree-shaking imperfect for some bundlers.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Modern bundlers tree-shake side-effect-free modules.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: side-effect-free flag must be set. Many libs don't.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Set sideEffects: false + provide both.

context_dependent_answer: Library: multiple entry points + sideEffects: false.
decision_framework: debate-188: judge the code splitting philosophy debate in this scenario: Library bundle: single file vs multiple entry points.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors single-file libs. Senior insight: tree-shaking depends on library structure.

scenario: Keyboard shortcuts: global registration vs scoped.
context: App with many keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+K search, Cmd+/ help, etc.).
sam_initial_position: Global registry. Centralized, easier to document.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Scoped means shortcut works only when relevant component mounted.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Global has issues - Cmd+K in input vs in app context.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Scope handles context naturally. Active component's shortcuts active.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Documentation? Global registry has list.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Scoped components publish their shortcuts to a registry for docs.

context_dependent_answer: Scoped + central registry for docs.
decision_framework: debate-189: judge the accessibility tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Keyboard shortcuts: global registration vs scoped.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors register globally. Senior insight: shortcuts have context. Input field's behavior shouldn't be hijacked.

scenario: Repository pattern for frontend data layer.
context: Should frontend have abstract 'repository' over fetch calls?
alex_initial_position: Yes. Repository pattern centralizes API concerns.
sam_initial_position: No. React Query is the abstraction. Repository over it is redundant.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Repository abstracts the URL. Backend changes don't ripple.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: React Query queryFn already abstracts the URL.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Repository can compose multiple endpoints, add headers, etc.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: queryFn can do all that. Repository is one extra layer.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For testing, repository can be mocked.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: MSW mocks at network. Better.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted. queryFn + MSW > repository.

context_dependent_answer: queryFn + MSW. Skip repository pattern.
decision_framework: debate-190: judge the frontend architecture patterns debate in this scenario: Repository pattern for frontend data layer.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors over-abstract. Senior insight: React Query is the abstraction. Don't wrap unnecessarily.

scenario: Immer vs immutable patterns by hand.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Hand-written for nested is verbose: {...state, items: state.items.map(...)}.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Verbosity is explicit. Immer hides what's happening.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Immer's produce pattern is well-known. Not hidden.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Bundle cost ~14KB.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Already in Redux Toolkit. Free if using RTK.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted with RTK. Without: hand-written.

context_dependent_answer: RTK: Immer (included). Other: hand-written or Immer if nested.
decision_framework: debate-191: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: Immer vs immutable patterns by hand.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors mutate state directly. Senior insight: immutability discipline. Immer makes it ergonomic.

scenario: Boolean prop explosion: refactor when?
context: <Button isPrimary isLarge isLoading isDisabled isRounded /> getting unwieldy.
alex_initial_position: Refactor at 5 booleans. Variants object pattern.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: 5 booleans = 32 combinations. Most invalid.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Some are valid (loading + disabled). Don't force into variant.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Style booleans (primary, large, rounded) merge to variants. State booleans (loading, disabled) stay.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed. Refactor style booleans to variants.

context_dependent_answer: Style: variants. State: booleans. Refactor when 5+ style booleans.
decision_framework: debate-192: judge the component composition patterns debate in this scenario: Boolean prop explosion: refactor when?. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors add another boolean. Senior insight: boolean prop sprawl is a design smell. Variants for style, booleans for state.

scenario: File upload form: progressive enhancement vs JS-required.
alex_initial_position: Progressive: <form action=...> works without JS, enhanced with JS.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Server actions support progressive enhancement.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Drag-drop, preview, progress need JS. Just require it.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Basic upload works without JS. Enhanced UX with JS.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Progressive baseline + JS enhancement.

context_dependent_answer: Progressive enhancement when possible. JS-required for rich features.
decision_framework: debate-193: judge the form library choices debate in this scenario: File upload form: progressive enhancement vs JS-required.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors require JS for everything. Senior insight: progressive enhancement is reliability.

scenario: GraphQL: fragments colocated with components vs centralized.
alex_initial_position: Colocated. Component owns its data shape.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Colocation matches React's component model.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Centralized makes 'all User queries' visible.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Tooling (codegen, relay) supports colocation.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Colocation with codegen.

context_dependent_answer: Colocate fragments with components. Use codegen.
decision_framework: debate-194: judge the data fetching strategy debate in this scenario: GraphQL: fragments colocated with components vs centralized.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors put queries in one file. Senior insight: GraphQL's value is precise per-component data fetching.

scenario: CSS Container Queries vs media queries for component responsiveness.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Container queries respond to container size, not viewport. Component reusable in any layout.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Browser support catching up but not universal.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: All major browsers support since 2023.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Container queries for reusable components.

context_dependent_answer: Reusable components: container queries. Layout: media queries.
decision_framework: debate-195: judge the styling approach wars debate in this scenario: CSS Container Queries vs media queries for component responsiveness.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use media queries everywhere. Senior insight: media queries are viewport-relative; container queries are component-relative.

scenario: Scroll-triggered animations: IntersectionObserver vs scroll-timeline.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: scroll-timeline runs on compositor. IO runs callback on main thread.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: scroll-timeline newer.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Chrome supports. Progressive enhancement.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Use scroll-timeline with IO fallback.

context_dependent_answer: Modern browsers: scroll-timeline. Fallback: IO.
decision_framework: debate-196: judge the animation library decisions debate in this scenario: Scroll-triggered animations: IntersectionObserver vs scroll-timeline.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors hook scroll events. Senior insight: native CSS scroll animations are compositor-driven, jank-free.

scenario: Test naming: 'should do X' vs 'does X when Y'.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: 'should' is filler. 'does X when Y' is clearer.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: 'should' is convention. Familiar.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: BDD specs use 'when/then'. Match that pattern.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. 'when X, does Y' clearest.

context_dependent_answer: 'when X, does Y' or 'X when Y'.
decision_framework: debate-197: judge the testing philosophy debate in this scenario: Test naming: 'should do X' vs 'does X when Y'.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors write 'tests Y'. Senior insight: test names are documentation. Behavioral specs read as requirements.

scenario: Discriminated unions with type guards vs Zod runtime parsing.
alex_initial_position: Zod parses + narrows in one step.
sam_initial_position: Hand-written type guards for known shapes.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Zod schema = source of truth + runtime check.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Type guard simpler for internal types.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For incoming data, Zod. Internal: type guards.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Incoming: Zod. Internal: type guards.
decision_framework: debate-198: judge the typescript strictness debate in this scenario: Discriminated unions with type guards vs Zod runtime parsing.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors check types with typeof. Senior insight: type guards narrow at compile time.

scenario: Image CDN vs Next/Vercel image optimization vs self-hosted IPX.
alex_initial_position: Image CDN (Cloudflare Images, Imgix). Pay per use, no infra.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: CDN scales infinitely. Next Image optimization at Vercel costs at scale.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Next Image is integrated. CDN is another service.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For high-volume, CDN cheaper. Low-volume: Next Image.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Self-hosted IPX for own control.

context_dependent_answer: Low volume: Next Image. High volume: CDN. Need control: self-host IPX.
decision_framework: debate-199: judge the performance vs dx tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Image CDN vs Next/Vercel image optimization vs self-hosted IPX.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't optimize images. Senior insight: images are biggest bytes. Optimization is critical.

scenario: TypeScript: tsc vs tsgo (Go-based TS compiler).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: tsgo 10x faster on large projects.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: tsc is reference. tsgo could lag features.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: tsgo aims feature-parity. Released staged.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Adopt when stable. Watch closely.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Agreed. Adopt when stable, fallback to tsc.

context_dependent_answer: Adopt tsgo when stable. Until then: tsc.
decision_framework: debate-200: judge the build tool choices debate in this scenario: TypeScript: tsc vs tsgo (Go-based TS compiler).. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors run tsc in dev. Senior insight: typecheck separate from build. Faster builds via build tool, typecheck via tsc.
